pages.full_text:
St. Peter's Melverley on the banks of R. Vyrnwy 1406

We shopped at the Craft Centre in the Old Rectory. We all walked across the old railway bridge, now a road bridge, to look at the pub, the Fir Tree, we had a beer + lunched at the van.
photo_captions: St. Peter's Melverley on the banks of R. Vyrnwy 1406
pages.notes: Caption '1406' appears to be the date of the church building, not a diary date. The caption is written vertically along the right edge of the photo. Text at bottom is cut off at the right gutter edge — continues on R page.

pages.full_text:
St. Peter's Melverley, Jacobean Pulpit, Chain Bible + CB seating.

After tea we walked up river, along the [?levees] built to contain the flooding. Char[les] sent Tonie into a hollow tree & then down a foxhole where he completely disappear[ed]
photo_captions: St. Peter's Melverley, Jacobean Pulpit, Chain Bible + CB seating.
pages.notes: The word after 'along the' is partially obscured — could read 'levees' or 'levees' (earthen flood embankments); text at bottom-right is cut off at page edge, 'disappear[ed]' is truncated. 'Char[les]' is partially cut off at left gutter — likely 'Charles'. 'CB seating' in caption may stand for 'box' seating or similar. The left gutter carries a continuation of the L-page caption: 'R. Vyrnwy 1406'.
